SEARCH
Package de.uka.ilkd.key.util.mergerule

Class MergeRuleUtils

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.util.mergerule.MergeRuleUtils
public class MergeRuleUtils extends Object
This class encapsulates static methods used in the MergeRule implementation. The methods are organized into different sections (see comments):

  1. SIMPLE AUXILIARIES
  2. GENERAL LOGIC
    1. Syntax
    2. Provability
    3. Simplification
    4. Calculus
  3. MERGE RELATED
  4. PRIVATE
Feel free to make private methods publicly visible if you need them.

    Constructors

    Description
    MergeRuleUtils()

    parsePlaceholder(String input, Services services)
    Parses a declaration of the type "<SORT> <NAME>", where <SORT> must be a sort known to the proof and <NAME> must be a fresh name.

  • Method Details

    • wrapOption

      public static <T> Optional<T> wrapOption(T obj)
      Wraps the given object s.th. if it is null, a None value is returned, and a Some value wrapping the object is returned otherwise.
      Parameters:
      obj - The object to wrap.
      Returns:
      None iff obj is null, Some(obj) otherwise.

    • stripIndex

      public static String stripIndex(String name)
      For Strings "xxx_i", this method returns "xxx". For Strings without the underscore, the original String is returned.
      Parameters:
      name - Name to remove the index from.
      Returns:
      The name without the index, if any.

      public static int intPow(int a, int b)
      Power function for integers.
      Parameters:
      a - The base.
      b - The exponent.
      Returns:
      a^b.

      Returns:
      An ArrayList containing exactly the given element.

      toTranslate - The formula to be translated.

    • getUpdateRightSideForSafe

      public static Optional<JTerm> getUpdateRightSideForSafe(JTerm update, LocationVariable leftSide)
      Returns the right side for a given location variable in an update (in normal form).
      Parameters:
      update - Update term to search.
      leftSide - Left side to find the right side for.
      Returns:
      The right side in the update for the given left side. Returns a None value if the right side could not be determined.

    • getUpdateRightSideFor

      public static JTerm getUpdateRightSideFor(JTerm update, LocationVariable leftSide)
      Returns the right side for a given location variable in an update (in normal form).
      Parameters:
      update - Update term to search.
      leftSide - Left side to find the right side for.
      Returns:
      The right side in the update for the given left side, or null if the right side could not be determined.

    • countAtoms

      public static int countAtoms(JTerm term)
      Counts the atoms in a formula.
      Parameters:
      term - Formula to count atoms for.
      Returns:
      Number of atoms in the formula
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the supplied term is not a formula

    • countDisjunctions

      public static int countDisjunctions(JTerm term, boolean negated)
      Counts the disjunctions in a formula.
      Parameters:
      term - Formula to count disjunctions for.
      negated - Set to true iff the current subformula is in the scope of a negation; in this case, "and" junctors have the role of "or" junctors considering the disjunctive complexity.
      Returns:
      Number of disjunctions in the formula
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the supplied term is not a formula

    • getNewSkolemConstantForPrefix

      public static Function getNewSkolemConstantForPrefix(String prefix, Sort sort, Services services)
      Computes and registers a new Skolem constant with the given prefix in its name of the given sort.
      Parameters:
      prefix - Prefix for the name of the constant.
      sort - Sort of the constant.
      services - The services object.
      Returns:
      A new Skolem constant of the given sort with the given prefix in its name.

    • getFreshVariableForPrefix

      public static LogicVariable getFreshVariableForPrefix(String prefix, Sort sort, Services services)
      Computes and registers a fresh variable with the given prefix in its name of the given sort.
      Parameters:
      prefix - Prefix for the name of the variable.
      sort - Sort of the variable.
      services - The services object.
      Returns:
      A fresh variable of the given sort with the given prefix in its name.

    • getFreshLocVariableForPrefix

      public static LocationVariable getFreshLocVariableForPrefix(String prefix, Sort sort, Services services)
      Computes and registers a fresh location variable with the given prefix in its name of the given sort.
      Parameters:
      prefix - Prefix for the name of the variable.
      sort - Sort of the variable.
      services - The services object.
      Returns:
      A fresh location variable of the given sort with the given prefix in its name.

    • substConstantsByFreshVars

      public static JTerm substConstantsByFreshVars(JTerm term, HashMap<Function,LogicVariable> replMap, Services services)
      Substitutes all constants in the given term by fresh variables. Multiple occurrences of a constant are substituted by the same variable.
      Parameters:
      term - Term in which to substitute constants by variables.
      replMap - Map from constants to variables in order to remember substitutions of one constant.
      Returns:
      A term equal to the input, but with constants substituted by fresh variables.

    • substConstantsByFreshVars

      public static JTerm substConstantsByFreshVars(JTerm term, HashSet<Function> restrictTo, HashMap<Function,LogicVariable> replMap, Services services)
      Substitutes all constants in the given term that are contained in the set restrictTo by fresh variables. If restrictTo is null, then all constants in the term are replaced. Multiple occurrences of a constant are substituted by the same variable.
      Parameters:
      term - Term in which to substitute constants by variables.
      restrictTo - Set of constants to replace. If null, all constants are replaced.
      replMap - Map from constants to variables in order to remember substitutions of one constant.
      Returns:
      A term equal to the input, but with constants substituted by fresh variables.

    • exClosure

      public static JTerm exClosure(JTerm term, Services services)
      Existentially closes all logical and location variables in the given term.
      Parameters:
      term - Term to existentially close.
      services - The services object.
      Returns:
      A new term which is equivalent to the existential closure of the argument term.

    • allClosure

      public static JTerm allClosure(JTerm term, Services services)
      Universally closes all logical and location variables in the given term.
      Parameters:
      term - Term to universally close.
      services - The services object.
      Returns:
      A new term which is equivalent to the universal closure of the argument term.

    • isUpdateNormalForm

      public static boolean isUpdateNormalForm(Term u)
      Checks if an update is of the form { x := v || ... || z := q}.
      Parameters:
      u - Update to check.
      Returns:
      true iff u is in normal form.

    • getConjunctiveElementsFor

      public static ArrayList<JTerm> getConjunctiveElementsFor(JTerm term)
      Dissects a conjunction into its conjunctive elements.
      Parameters:
      term - Conjunctive formula to dissect (may be a conjunction of one element, i.e. no "real" conjunction). In this case, the resulting list will contain exactly the supplied formula.
      Returns:
      The conjunctive elements of the supplied formula.

    • getBranchUniqueLocVar

      public static LocationVariable getBranchUniqueLocVar(LocationVariable var, Node startLeaf)
      Find a location variable for the given one that is unique for the branch corresponding to the given goal, but not necessarily globally unique. The variable with the first branch-unique name w.r.t. a numeric index is returned.
      Parameters:
      var - Variable to get a branch-unique correspondent for.
      startLeaf - The leaf of the branch.
      Returns:
      The first indexed PV that is unique w.r.t. the given branch, but not with the global variable registry.

    • getIntroducingNodeforLocVar

      public static Node getIntroducingNodeforLocVar(LocationVariable var, Node node)
      Finds the node, from the given leaf on, where the variable was introduced.
      Parameters:
      var - Variable to find introducing node for.
      node - Leaf to start from.
      Returns:
      The node where the variable was introduced.

    • getJavaBlockRecursive

      public static JavaBlock getJavaBlockRecursive(JTerm term)
      Returns the first Java block in the given term that can be found by recursive search, or the empty block if there is no non-empty Java block in the term.
      Parameters:
      term - The term to extract Java blocks for.
      Returns:
      The first Java block in the given term or the empty block if there is no non-empty Java block.

    • isProvable

      public static boolean isProvable(JTerm toProve, Services services, int timeout)
      Tries to prove the given formula without splitting and returns whether the prove could be closed.
      Parameters:
      toProve - Formula to prove.
      services - The services object.
      timeout - Time in milliseconds after which the side proof is aborted.
      Returns:
      True iff the given formula has been successfully proven.

    • isProvableWithSplitting

      public static boolean isProvableWithSplitting(JTerm toProve, Services services, int timeout)
      Tries to prove the given formula with splitting and returns whether the prove could be closed.
      Parameters:
      toProve - Formula to prove.
      services - The services object.
      timeout - Time in milliseconds after which the side proof is aborted.
      Returns:
      True iff the given formula has been successfully proven.

    • isProvable

      public static boolean isProvable(Sequent toProve, Services services, int timeout)
      Tries to prove the given formula without splitting and returns whether the prove could be closed.
      Parameters:
      toProve - Sequent to prove.
      services - The services object.
      timeout - Time in milliseconds after which the side proof is aborted.
      Returns:
      True iff the given formula has been successfully proven.

    • isProvableWithSplitting

      public static boolean isProvableWithSplitting(Sequent toProve, Services services, int timeout)
      Tries to prove the given formula with splitting and returns whether the prove could be closed.
      Parameters:
      toProve - Sequent to prove.
      services - The services object.
      timeout - Time in milliseconds after which the side proof is aborted.
      Returns:
      True iff the given formula has been successfully proven.

    • assertEquivalent

      public static void assertEquivalent(JTerm term1, JTerm term2, Services services, int timeout)
      Tries to prove the equivalence of term1 and term2 and throws a RuntimeException if the proof fails.
      Parameters:
      term1 - First term to check.
      term2 - Second term to check.
      services - The services object.
      timeout - Time in milliseconds after which the side proof is aborted.
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - iff proving the equivalence of term1 and term2 fails.

    • trySimplify

      public static JTerm trySimplify(Proof parentProof, JTerm term, boolean countDisjunctions, int timeout)
      Tries to simplifies the given JTerm in a side proof with splits. If this attempt is successful, i.e. the number of atoms in the simplified formula is lower (and, if requested, also the number of disjunctions), the simplified formula is returned; otherwise, the original formula is returned.

      Please note that using this method can consume a great amount of time!

      Parameters:
      parentProof - The parent Proof.
      term - The JTerm to simplify.
      countDisjunctions - If set to true, the method also takes the number of disjunctions (in addition to the number of atoms) into account when judging about the complexity of the "simplified" formula.
      timeout - Time in milliseconds after which the side proof is aborted.
      Returns:
      The simplified JTerm or the original term, if simplification was not successful.
      See Also:
      • simplify(Proof, JTerm, int)

    • clearSemisequent

      public static void clearSemisequent(Goal goal, boolean antec)
      Deletes all formulae of the succedent / antecedent.
      Parameters:
      goal - Goal to delete formulae from.
      antec - If true, antecedent formulae are deleted, else succedent formulae.

    • equalsModBranchUniqueRenaming

      public static boolean equalsModBranchUniqueRenaming(SourceElement se1, SourceElement se2, Node node, Services services)
      An equals method that, before the comparison, replaces all program locations in the supplied arguments by their branch-unique versions.
      Parameters:
      se1 - First element to check equality (mod renaming) for.
      se2 - Second element to check equality (mod renaming) for.
      node - The node of the current branch (for getting branch-unique names).
      services - The Services object.
      Returns:
      true iff source elements can be matched, considering branch-unique location names.

    • createSimplifiedDisjunctivePathCondition

      public static JTerm createSimplifiedDisjunctivePathCondition(JTerm cond1, JTerm cond2, Services services, int simplificationTimeout)
      Creates a path condition that is equivalent to the disjunction of the two supplied formulae, but possibly simpler. In the ideal case, the returned formula can be literally shorter than each of the two formulae; in this case, it consists of the common elements of those.

      The underlying idea is based upon the observation that many path conditions that should be merged are conjunctions of mostly the same elements and, in addition, formulae phi and !phi that vanish after creating the disjunction of the path conditions. The corresponding valid formula is (phi & psi) | (phi & !psi) <-> phi

      For formulae that cannot be simplified by this law, the method performs two additional steps:
      (1) it applies, if possible, distributivity to simplify the result
      (2) it checks whether the disjunction is already equivalent to the common parts of the formulae only. This often happens when merging all branches that occur in symbolic execution.

      Parameters:
      cond1 - First path condition to merge.
      cond2 - Second path condition to merge.
      services - The services object.
      simplificationTimeout - Time in milliseconds after which the side proof is aborted.
      Returns:
      A path condition that is equivalent to the disjunction of the two supplied formulae, but possibly simpler.

    • getDistinguishingFormula

      public static Optional<Pair<JTerm,JTerm>> getDistinguishingFormula(JTerm pathCondition1, JTerm pathCondition2, Services services)
      Computes a formula that implies pathCondition1 and, if pathCondition1 and pathCondition2 are contradicting, does not imply pathCondition2. The computed formula is at most as complex as pathCondition1. This so generated distinguishing formula is returned in the first element of the pair; the "rest" is contained in the second. It always holds that the conjunction of the first element of the pair and the second element of the pair is equivalent to pathCondition1.
      Parameters:
      pathCondition1 - The first formula to compute a distinguishing formula for.
      pathCondition2 - The second formula to compute a distinguishing formula for.
      services - The services object.
      Returns:
      (1) A formula that implies pathCondition1 and, if pathCondition1 and pathCondition2 are contradicting, does not imply pathCondition2, and (2) the "rest" of pathCondition1 that is common with pathCondition2.

    • getDistinguishingFormula

      public static Optional<Pair<JTerm,JTerm>> getDistinguishingFormula(ArrayList<JTerm> conjElemsPathCond1, ArrayList<JTerm> conjElemsPathCond2, Services services)
      See Also:
      • getDistinguishingFormula(JTerm, JTerm, Services)

    • pathConditionsAreDistinguishable

      public static boolean pathConditionsAreDistinguishable(JTerm pathCondition1, JTerm pathCondition2, Services services)
      Checks if two given path conditions are distinguishable.
      Parameters:
      pathCondition1 - First path condition to check.
      pathCondition2 - Second path condition to check.
      services - The services object.
      Returns:
      True iff the two given path conditions are distinguishable.

    • closeMergePartnerGoal

      public static void closeMergePartnerGoal(Node mergeNodeParent, Goal mergePartner, PosInOccurrence pio, SymbolicExecutionState mergeState, SymbolicExecutionState mergePartnerState, JTerm pc, Set<Name> newNames)
      Closes the given partner goal, using the CloseAfterMerge rule.
      Parameters:
      mergeNodeParent - Parent of remaining join node.
      mergePartner - Partner goal to close.
      newNames - The set of new names (of Skolem constants) introduced in the merge.

    • sequentToSEPair

      public static SymbolicExecutionState sequentToSEPair(Node node, PosInOccurrence pio, Services services)
      Converts a sequent (given by Goal and PosInOccurrence) to an SE state (U,C). Thereby, all program variables occurring in the symbolic state are replaced by branch-unique correspondents in order to enable merging of different branches declaring local variables.

      Parameters:
      node - Current node.
      pio - Position of update-program counter formula in goal.
      services - The services object.
      Returns:
      An SE state (U,C).
      See Also:
      • sequentToSETriple(Node, PosInOccurrence, Services)

    • sequentToSETriple

      public static SymbolicExecutionStateWithProgCnt sequentToSETriple(Node node, PosInOccurrence pio, Services services)
      Converts a sequent (given by Goal and PosInOccurrence) to an SE state (U,C,p). Thereby, all program variables occurring in the program counter and in the symbolic state are replaced by branch-unique correspondents in order to enable merging of different branches declaring local variables.

      The problem which makes this renaming necessary is the fact that when executing a program like int x; x = ..., the variable x is renamed to x_1, x_2 and so on in different branches, which makes a "normal" merging impossible. Branch unique names are tracked in the LocationVariables when they are renamed in InnerVariableNamer. Soundness is not effected by the switch to branch-unique names. However, merged nodes are then of course potentially different from their predecessors concerning the involved local variable symbols.

      Parameters:
      node - Current node.
      pio - Position of update-program counter formula in goal.
      services - The services object.
      Returns:
      An SE state (U,C,p).

    • sequentsToSEPairs

      public static ImmutableList<SymbolicExecutionState> sequentsToSEPairs(Iterable<MergePartner> sequentInfos)
      Convenience method for converting a whole list of goal-pio combinations to symbolic execution states; relies on sequentToSETriple(Node, PosInOccurrence, Services).
      Parameters:
      sequentInfos - Goals and PosInOccurrences specifying merge partners and the positions of the program counter-post condition formulae in the goals.
      Returns:
      A list of symbolic execution states.

    • handleNameClashes

      public static Pair<SymbolicExecutionState,SymbolicExecutionState> handleNameClashes(SymbolicExecutionState mergeState, SymbolicExecutionState mergePartnerState, Services services)
      Due to the introduction of local namespaces, we run into trouble when applying state merging in the presence of locally introduced symbols. These include program variables as well as Skolem constants. This method finds all name clashes and renames the corresponding entities in the SymbolicExecutionState state.
      Parameters:
      mergeState - The SymbolicExecutionState in which the partners should be merged.
      mergePartnerState - The SymbolicExecutionState of the second merge partner.
      services - The Services object.
      Returns:
      The renamed SymbolicExecutionState of the second merge partner.

    • parsePlaceholder

      public static Pair<Sort,Name> parsePlaceholder(String input, Services services)
      Parses a declaration of the type "<SORT> <NAME>", where <SORT> must be a sort known to the proof and <NAME> must be a fresh name. This method is used, for instance, in the GUI dialog for predicate abstraction. The parsed placeholder is registered in KeY's namespaces.
      Parameters:
      input - Input to parse.
      services - The services object.
      Returns:
      A pair of parsed sort and name for the placeholder.
      Throws:
      de.uka.ilkd.key.util.mergerule.MergeRuleUtils.NameAlreadyBoundException - If the given placeholder is already known to the system.
      de.uka.ilkd.key.util.mergerule.MergeRuleUtils.SortNotKnownException - If the given sort is not known to the system.

    • parsePlaceholder

      public static Pair<Sort,Name> parsePlaceholder(String input, boolean registerInNamespaces, Services services)
      Parses a declaration of the type "<SORT> <NAME>", where <SORT> must be a sort known to the proof and <NAME> must be a fresh name. This method is used, for instance, in the GUI dialog for predicate abstraction. The parsed placeholder is registered in KeY's namespaces iff registerInNamespaces is true.
      Parameters:
      input - Input to parse.
      registerInNamespaces - Flag to indicate whether the parsed placeholder should be registered in the namespaces.
      services - The services object.
      Returns:
      A pair of parsed sort and name for the placeholder.
      Throws:
      de.uka.ilkd.key.util.mergerule.MergeRuleUtils.NameAlreadyBoundException - If the given placeholder is already known to the system.
      de.uka.ilkd.key.util.mergerule.MergeRuleUtils.SortNotKnownException - If the given sort is not known to the system.

    • parsePredicate

      public static AbstractionPredicate parsePredicate(String input, ArrayList<Pair<Sort,Name>> registeredPlaceholders, NamespaceSet localNamespaces, Services services) throws ParserException
      Parses an abstraction predicate. The parameter input should be a textual representation of a formula containing exactly one of the also supplied placeholders (but the contained placeholder may have multiple occurrences in the formula). The result is an abstraction predicate mapping terms of the type of the placeholder to the parsed predicate with the placeholder substituted by the argument term.
      Parameters:
      input - The predicate to parse (contains exactly one placeholder).
      localNamespaces - The local NamespaceSet.
      Returns:
      The parsed AbstractionPredicate.
      Throws:
      ParserException - If there is a syntax error.

    • rename

      public static LocationVariable rename(Name newName, LocationVariable lv)
      Returns an equivalent variable with the new name.
      Parameters:
      newName - the new name
      lv - the location variable to be renamed
      Returns:
      equivalent operator with the new name
